data.content:
# Implementation Plan: Make setup CLI additive (Revision 6)

## Prior Rejection Gaps — All Addressed

| # | Gap | Fix |
|---|-----|-----|
| 1 | TOML parser assumed shell-writer format only | Reader handles both shell-writer (triple-quote multiline) and smol-toml (escaped single-line) output |
| 2 | REGISTERED_AGENTS=() resets not removed | Step 4 deletes setup.sh:930 and agent-wizard.sh:499 |
| 3 | Dispatch editing not additive | Steps 5-8 show current mappings/pools with (current) markers and Keep current defaults |
| 4 | Detected agents filtered by existing registrations | Step 4 does NOT filter FOUND_AGENTS; all detected agents shown; model menus show all available models |
| 5 | Tests lacked interactive verification | Step 12B adds PTY-driven tests that seed settings, run setup, add one item, assert everything else preserved |
| 6 | backend.type, defaults.profileId, terminalLightTheme lost | Steps 1 and 10 read/write all scalar settings from/to KV store |
| 7 | Agent provider/flavor/version fields dropped | Step 10d writes pass-through agent fields |

## Step 1: Add _read_existing_settings() to setup.sh

Location: After KV helpers (after line 162), before _slugify(). Pure-bash state-machine TOML reader (~100 lines). Reads $_AGENT_SETTINGS_FILE line by line. No external dependencies.

State machine states: top (scalars), agents (inside [agents.<id>]), actions, backend, defaults, scope (inside [scopeRefinement]), scope_multiline (triple-quoted body), pools (empty arrays), pools_aot (inside [[pools.<step>]]).

What it populates:
- REGISTERED_AGENTS array with each agent ID from [agents.*]
- _kv_set AGENT_COMMANDS/LABELS/MODELS/PROVIDERS/FLAVORS/VERSIONS per agent
- _kv_set ACTION_MAP take/scene/breakdown/scopeRefinement
- _kv_set BACKEND type, DEFAULTS profileId/max_concurrent_sessions/max_claims_per_queue_type
- _kv_set DISPATCH dispatch_mode
- _TERMINAL_LIGHT_THEME variable (boolean)
- _SCOPE_PROMPT variable (multiline string)
- _kv_set POOL_COUNT/POOL_AGENT_*/POOL_WEIGHT_* for advanced pools

TOML parsing handles: section headers [agents.id], array-of-tables [[pools.step]], string key = "value", integers, booleans, multiline key = """...""", escaped single-line strings (printf %b for \n), empty arrays key = []. Graceful fallback: returns 0 if settings file missing.

## Step 2: Add identical _read_existing_settings() to agent-wizard.sh

Same logic referencing $SETTINGS_FILE. Loads ALL settings so writer preserves them. Follows existing parallel implementation pattern.

## Step 3: Make _register_agent_entry() dedup-aware

Files: setup.sh:761-767, agent-wizard.sh:320-326. Check if ID already in REGISTERED_AGENTS. If exists, update metadata (command, label, model) without re-appending. If new, append normally. Pre-loaded agents from Step 1 stay in array.

## Step 4: Remove REGISTERED_AGENTS=() resets; keep all detected agents visible

4a. Delete setup.sh:930 and agent-wizard.sh:499 (the REGISTERED_AGENTS=() lines).
4b. Show existing agents before scanning. Change scan prompt to "Scan for additional agents?" when agents exist.
4c. Do NOT filter FOUND_AGENTS. All detected agents always shown. If codex-gpt-5 is registered and codex detected, user sees full codex model menu and can add codex-gpt-5-2.
4d. Single-agent case: register with dedup, only assign action mappings if none exist yet.

## Step 5: Update _prompt_action_choice() to show current value

Files: setup.sh:726-748, agent-wizard.sh:289-311. Add current_agent parameter. Show (current) marker, default to current agent index, Enter keeps existing mapping.

## Step 6: Make _prompt_action_mappings() pass current values

Pass _kv_get ACTION_MAP value as current_agent to _prompt_action_choice.

## Step 7: Make _dispatch_wizard() show Keep current option

When config exists: 1) Keep current, 2) Simple, 3) Advanced [default 1]. Fresh install: original 2-choice menu.

## Step 8: Make _prompt_single_pool() additive

When pool has entries: 1) Keep current, 2) Reconfigure, 0) Clear. Other pools untouched.

## Step 9: Make _defaults_wizard() use current values as defaults

Read from KV store instead of hardcoded 5/10. Scope prompt already reads from _SCOPE_PROMPT.

## Step 10: Update _write_settings_toml() in both scripts

10a. backend.type from _kv_get BACKEND type "auto" (not hardcoded)
10b. defaults.profileId from _kv_get DEFAULTS profileId "" (not hardcoded)
10c. Add terminalLightTheme output after maxClaimsPerQueueType
10d. Write agent provider/flavor/version fields from KV when non-empty
10e. agent-wizard.sh: full pool writer replacing empty-pools-only writer

## Step 11: Wire up entry points

11a. setup.sh foolery_setup(): call _read_existing_settings before while loop. Mark steps [configured].
11b. agent-wizard.sh maybe_agent_wizard(): call _read_existing_settings before scan prompt.

## Step 12: Tests

### A. Unit tests in setup-settings-writer.test.ts (8 new tests)

1. reader seeds agents into REGISTERED_AGENTS
2. reader seeds all scalar settings (non-default values)
3. reader seeds scope refinement prompt (multi-line triple-quoted)
4. reader seeds smol-toml escaped prompt (single-line with \n)
5. reader seeds pool config (advanced mode [[pools.step]])
6. load-then-write round-trip preserves all settings (smol-toml write -> read -> write -> parse -> assert)
7. preserves existing agents when adding new ones (seed 2 + add 1 = 3)
8. dedup on re-register (same ID, updated model = 1 entry)

### B. PTY test: scripts/test-setup-additive.sh

Follows test-install-copilot-setup.sh pattern. Seed: 2 agents, action mappings, backend.type=knots, defaults.profileId=guided, terminalLightTheme=true, maxConcurrentSessions=8, maxClaimsPerQueueType=15.

Run 1: Add claude-sonnet variant via Agents wizard. Assert all existing preserved + new variant added.
Run 2: Change one dispatch mapping. Assert all agents present, change applied, everything else unchanged.

### C. PTY test: scripts/test-agent-wizard-additive.sh

Seed 2 agents + non-default backend/defaults. Run maybe_agent_wizard, add variant. Assert preservation.

### D. Register in package.json as test:smoke:setup:additive and test:smoke:wizard:additive

## Complexity: Medium

~200 lines new bash, ~80 lines UX, ~30 lines writer, ~400 lines tests. Risk: bash TOML parsing mitigated by round-trip tests and constrained format. No new deps. Fresh installs unchanged.

## File Changes

| File | Type | Lines |
|------|------|-------|
| scripts/setup.sh | Modified | +180 |
| scripts/agent-wizard.sh | Modified | +150 |
| setup-settings-writer.test.ts | Modified | +200 |
| test-setup-additive.sh | New | ~100 |
| test-agent-wizard-additive.sh | New | ~60 |
| package.json | Modified | +2 |
